headline: Is General Mills’ (GIS) Clean‑Label Cereal Push Quietly Redefining Its Brand Investment Narrative?
summary: In March 2026, General Mills launched two new cereals, Lucky Charms Unicorn Cotton Candy and Tropical Trix featuring Disney’s Moana, made with colors from natural sources and aligned with its plan to remove certified colors from its U.S. cereal portfolio by summer 2026 and full U.S. retail range by the end of 2027. This push combines playful, licensed branding with a cleaner-label commitment, highlighting how General Mills is trying to refresh core cereal brands while shifting its ingredient...

headline: This Stock Yields 6.6% and Has a 127-Year Streak of Never Cutting Its Dividend. Here's Why It's a Buy Now.
summary: General Mills' dividend has been reliably paid for several generations.

headline: At a 15-Year Low and Yielding 6.7%, General Mills Is a Bargain for Income Investors
summary: General Mills (NYSE:GIS) is one of America’s most enduring food giants, with roots stretching back to the 19th century and a portfolio of iconic brands including Cheerios, Betty Crocker, Pillsbury, Yoplait, and Blue Buffalo pet foods. The company has paid dividends without interruption for 127 consecutive years — a rare feat that underscores its stability ... At a 15-Year Low and Yielding 6.7%, General Mills Is a Bargain for Income Investors

headline: Food Megamergers Hardly Ever Work. Could McCormick-Unilever Be Different?
summary: Combining with Unilever’s food division would remake McCormick into a global powerhouse, but the recent history of Big Food mergers hits a cautionary note.
